output:
CVE-2021-24909 is a vulnerability identified in the ACF Photo Gallery Field WordPress plugin versions prior to 1.7.5. It arises due to insufficient sanitization and escaping of the 'post' parameter in the 'includes/acf_photo_gallery_metabox_edit.php' file, which can lead to Reflected Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). This type of vulnerability is classified under CWE-79, indicating failure to appropriately neutralize user-controllable input.

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as medium. Exploitation of this flaw could allow attackers to execute arbitrary scripts in the context of a user's session, potentially leading to unauthorized actions on behalf of the user, such as account hijacking or phishing.

The affected software includes any versions of the ACF Photo Gallery Field plugin before 1.7.5 on WordPress platforms. To mitigate this issue, it is recommended to update the plugin to version 1.7.5 or later. Additionally, employing techniques such as input validation and output encoding can help prevent XSS vulnerabilities. The associated MITRE ATT&CK technique is T1190.
